title: Piquant Flaky Italian Chicken Thighs Casserole
description: A sour and cozy Italian dish featuring chicken thighs, inspired by Ligurian cuisine, with a double-fry method similar to KFC, and low-sodium friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the flour, cornstarch, and a pinch of salt-free seasoning blend.
Dredge the chicken thighs in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the chicken thighs until golden brown, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the fried chicken thighs to a baking dish and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the grated Parmesan cheese and cook until melted and slightly browned.
Pour the melted cheese over the fried chicken thighs and sprinkle with garlic powder and dried oregano.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the top is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and drizzle with lemon juice.
Let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
